Volcano plot for DStressR promoter-compound hits

Source: R/plots.R
plot_volcano.Rd

Creates a standard volcano plot from a DStressR result table. The x-axis is a promoter-compound effect size and the y-axis is the negative log10 adjusted p-value. Significant hits are emphasized, top promoter groups can be colored, and the most significant promoter-compound pairs are annotated.

Usage

plot_volcano(
  table,
  effect = "specific_effect",
  padj = "specific_padj",
  pvalue = NULL,
  promoter = "promoter",
  compound = "compound",
  compound_label = compound,
  fdr = 0.05,
  lfc = 0,
  top_n = 12,
  top_promoters = 6,
  title = "DStressR volcano plot",
  subtitle = NULL,
  xlab = NULL,
  ylab = NULL,
  label_by = c("pair", "promoter", "compound"),
  max_label_chars = 46,
  repel_labels = TRUE,
  point_alpha = 0.65
)

Arguments

table

A data frame with one row per promoter-compound pair.

effect

Effect-size column to plot on the x-axis.

padj

Adjusted p-value column to plot on the y-axis.

pvalue

Optional raw p-value column used only if padj = NULL.

promoter, compound

Columns identifying the promoter and compound.

compound_label

Optional column with human-readable compound names used for annotations. Defaults to compound.

fdr

FDR threshold for hit highlighting.

lfc

Minimum absolute effect size for hit highlighting.

top_n

Number of significant pairs to annotate.

top_promoters

Number of promoter groups to color. Remaining promoters are shown in grey.

title, subtitle

Plot title and subtitle.

xlab, ylab

Axis labels. Defaults to readable labels based on the selected columns.

label_by

Label style for annotated points. The default, "pair", labels top hits as promoter-compound pairs.

max_label_chars

Maximum characters per annotation label. Longer labels are truncated with .... Use Inf to keep full labels.

repel_labels

If TRUE and the optional ggrepel package is installed, use repelled labels for the annotated top hits.

point_alpha

Point transparency.

Value

A ggplot object.

Details

The defaults work with results() followed by adjust_pvalues() or call_hits(). For workflow comparison tables, pass the corresponding column names, for example effect = "destress_eb_effect_centered", padj = "estimated_alpha_eb_padj_by_promoter", compound = "srn_code", and compound_label = "ProductName".
